Home / Philosophy of AI

Branch Guide

Philosophy of AI

Truthfulness, prompting, capabilities, bias, public discourse, and the implications of dialoguing with machine systems.

Why This Branch Matters

machine reasoning, prompting, truth-alignment, public trust, and the ethics of human-machine dialogue.

Philosophy of AI matters here because the archive itself is built through human-machine dialogue. The branch therefore has to examine truthfulness, agency, prompting, bias, and responsibility from the inside.

A strong route through this branch asks what the model is doing, what the human is doing, and where the final responsibility for judgment belongs.

Core Tensions

The branch becomes useful when these pressures stay visible.

  • Truthfulness and hallucination
  • Prompting as epistemic design
  • Model bias and user bias
  • Human responsibility for final judgment

Entry Points
